# foreignObject label emit semantics & CI honesty — is scriba's own honesty machinery blind to FOs?

> **STATUS: CLOSED (implemented in 0.22.1-dev, 2026-07-03).** The fix
> landed structurally: KaTeX advance-sum measurement (`_math_metrics.py` +
> baked `katex_advances.json`), `measure_label_line` composer routed into
> all 14 heuristic call sites, per-line FO `overflow:visible`, single-line
> flex wrapper removed, `.scriba-annot-label` font pinned, FO-aware extent
> parsers, `SCRIBA_VERSION` 13→14. Regression pins: browser-truth table in
> `tests/unit/test_math_metrics.py`, emit contract in
> `tests/unit/test_fo_sizing.py::TestFolabelEmitContract`.


> **STATUS: INVESTIGATION 2026-07-03** — scriba v0.22.0 @ HEAD (a04bd9b), SCRIBA_VERSION 13.
> Design investigation; **no repo source modified**. Probes ran in the scratchpad against the
> installed `.venv` (py3.10). Visual rendered with local headless chromium against the real
> `scriba-annot-label` CSS. Builds on the pinned-font work
> (`investigations/text-width-architecture.md`) — which pins **14 px cell/node text only**; the
> annotation-label surface investigated here is still on the *unpinned* em-heuristic.

---

## 1. Hand-off Brief

**The suspicion, confirmed.** scriba reserves annotation-label space by a self-checking
"exactness" contract: the reservation runs the real annotation emitters into a scratch buffer
and measures the painted extent, so *"reserved space equals painted space by construction and
cannot drift from the renderer"* (`scriba/animation/primitives/_extent.py:13-17`). That contract
has a hole exactly where the reported bug lives: **the painted-extent parser cannot see
`<foreignObject>` at all**, and math labels are the one surface whose correctness is decided by
foreignObject text geometry. The honesty machinery is measuring the wrong rectangle.

**The mechanism (math label → clip).** A math annotation label (`$…$`) is emitted as one
`<foreignObject>` per line hosting KaTeX HTML, with the div pinned
`white-space:nowrap;overflow:hidden` (`_svg_helpers.py:1560-1575`, `_emit_pill_label_text`). The
box width `box_w` is the heuristic `pill_w` from `pill_dimensions`
(`_svg_helpers.py:1386-1415`) = `estimate_text_width(_label_width_text(line)) + pad`. The browser
lays the real text with `600 11px ui-monospace` (`scriba-scene-primitives.css:94`) — an
**unpinned** system font — plus KaTeX's proportional math fonts. When the real advance exceeds
the 0.62 em estimate, the text is **clipped, with no change to any SVG geometry**. On this Mac
nothing clips; the mechanism is real and per-machine.

**Why the render gate passes broken output (two blindness layers, both proven):**
1. **Parser layer** — both extent parsers match only
   `g|rect|circle|polygon|polyline|line|path` (`_extent.py:28`, `tests/helpers/painted_extent.py:23`).
   `foreignObject` is absent. Stripping every FO from a real emit leaves the measured extent
   *byte-identical* (Probe 1).
2. **Emit layer** — the reservation's measure hook `_measure_emit` (`base.py:448-461`) and the
   honesty tests' `prim.emit_svg()` (`test_painted_within_bbox.py:34`) both run **without**
   `render_inline_tex`, so math labels fall back to plain `<text>` and **no foreignObject is ever
   emitted into anything that gets measured** (Probe 3a). The "math" honesty cases exercise the
   plain-text fallback, not the FO path.

**The graded verdict:** the reported clip is invisible to CI **by architecture**, not by
oversight — see §2. Separately, the extent parser's FO-blindness is a **latent** correctness trap
(§3): an over-sized FO box escapes `painted ⊆ bbox` silently, though nothing triggers it in the
corpus **today**.

**What this doc settles:** (§2) FO-blindness verdict + graded evidence; (§3) what changes if the
parsers ingest FO rects, incl. the corpus violation sweep; (§4) clip-vs-grow with rendered
visuals; (§5) a two-tier gate (browserless FO-rect pin + opt-in browser clip probe); (§6) blast
radius and landing order.

**Probe artifacts** (scratchpad, re-runnable):
`probe1_foblind.py`, `probe2_escape_and_foaware.py`, `probe3_double_blind_and_others.py`,
`probe4_clip_tautology.py`, `clip_vs_grow.html` (+ chromium screenshot).

---

## 2. FO-blindness verdict, with probe evidence (the CI hole, graded)

### 2.1 Where the reserved lane learns FO extents today — it doesn't; it learns the *rect*

The reservation path is:

```
annotation_height_above()      base.py:367   ceil(-min_y) of…
 └ _annotation_extent()        base.py:391   measure_painted_extent(scratch)
    └ _measure_emit(parts)     base.py:448   emit_annotation_arrows(...)  ← NO render_inline_tex
       └ measure_painted_extent  _extent.py:129   regex over g/rect/circle/…/path
```

The white pill **background `<rect>`** is emitted separately at `_svg_helpers.py:2174-2181` with
`width="{pill_w}"`, and the FO (when present) is sized to the same `pill_w` and drawn inside it.
So the lane is reserved from the **rect**, which is self-consistent with itself by construction —
and completely independent of whether the FO's *text* fits. The FO box contributes nothing:

```
Probe 1a — grid, multi-line math arc label, emit WITH render_inline_tex:
  foreignObject count in emit           : 2
  tags the parser's _TAG_RE matches     : ['circle','g','path','polygon','polyline','rect']
  'foreignObject' in parser tag set     : False
  painted extent WITH foreignObjects    : (0.75, 1.0 .. 313.25, 169.25)
  painted extent with FOs STRIPPED OUT  : (0.75, 1.0 .. 313.25, 169.25)   ← IDENTICAL
  FO boxes (x,y,w,h) : [(1,133,312,18),(1,151,312,18)]   pill rect: [(1,127,312,42)]
```

```
Probe 1b / 3a — the measurement & test paths never even emit an FO:
  foreignObject in _measure_emit buffer : 0     (<text>: 1, white <rect>: 1)
  test's emit_svg() [no tex]            : 0 foreignObject
  emit_svg(render_inline_tex=…)         : 1 foreignObject
```

### 2.2 The escape: an over/under-sized FO passes every honesty test

Widen every emitted FO 3× (models a heuristic that under-shoots the real font, or any future
`box_w`/`pill_w` drift) so the box now spills far past the declared bbox:

```
Probe 2a — array, $\frac{a}{b}$ tổng position=above, bbox 308×66:
  after widening every FO 3× (spills right of bbox):
    FO-BLIND extent : max_x 307.0  → painted-within-bbox VERDICT: PASS (escaped!)
    FO-AWARE extent : max_x 321.0  → painted-within-bbox VERDICT: FAIL (caught: +13px right)
```

The FO-blind pin certifies a label that paints 13 px outside the box. This is the correctness
gap: `painted ⊆ bbox` is enforced on a rectangle the label may not actually occupy.

### 2.3 Grade

| Facet | Grade | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Blindness to the **reported clip** (text overflowing a correctly-placed FO box) | **Architectural / total** | The contract explicitly excludes text (`painted_extent.py:5-7`: *"Text is not measured; every annotation label sits inside its measured pill rect"*). For FO labels that premise is **false under a font miss** — the text is exactly what overflows. No rect-based measure, present or FO-aware, can see it (§3.2). Root cause: unpinned label font (§4). |
| Blindness to an **over-sized FO box** (`box_w` ≠ `pill_w`, or a non-rect-backed FO) | **Medium, latent** | Real and silent (2.2), but **dormant**: today `box_w := pill_w` so 0/6 corpus cases escape (§3.1). Becomes HIGH the moment box sizing diverges from the pill, or a new FO emitter ships without a backing rect. |
| Honesty of the stated exactness claim | **Overstated** | *"cannot drift from the renderer"* holds only for stroke geometry. The renderer's true paint for FO labels is browser text layout, which the measurer never observes. |

**Bottom line:** the reported bug is a bigger, quieter story than "a label clipped on one
machine" — the exactness contract's own words ("reserved == painted, cannot drift") do not hold
for the entire foreignObject label class, and CI is structurally unable to notice.

---

## 3. What FO-aware extents change (corpus violations found)

I built an FO-aware twin of `tests/helpers/painted_extent.py` (adds a `<foreignObject>` branch
folding `x/y/width/height` into the extent, same translate-stack) and swept a math-label battery.

### 3.1 Annotation-label battery — no box escapes today (good news for churn)

```
Probe 2b — 6 math-label cases (array/grid/dptable, multi- & single-line, arc & position):
  every case: blind outside(L,R,T,B) == aware outside(L,R,T,B) == (0,0,0,0)
  → 0 / 6 with FO box outside bbox TODAY
```

Because `box_w := pill_w` and the FO nests inside the pill rect, the FO-aware pin **agrees** with
the current pins on annotation labels. So turning the parser FO-aware **does not flip any existing
annotation-label pin red.** (It only bites when a box is mis-sized — 2.2 — which is the point.)

### 3.2 The other FO emitters — one *would* flip, and it's a false positive to guard against

```
Probe 3b — non-annotation FO emitters (overflow:visible), emit WITH tex:
  grid_math_cell_value        aware outside: (0,0,0,0)   ok
  graph_math_node_label       aware outside: (0,0,0,0)   ok
  numberline_math_tick        aware outside: (0,0,0,0)   ok
  metricplot_long_math_xlabel aware outside: (0, 2.0, 0, 2.0)   ← FO box exits bbox +2px R/B
```

The metricplot axis-label FO is deliberately `overflow:visible` — the "halo-for-overflow" design
that lets math axis/node labels spill exactly like their plain-`<text>` twins
(`_text_render.py:330-345`, `test_fo_sizing.py:45-57`). Its **box** legitimately pokes 2 px past
the bbox. A naïve FO-aware `painted ⊆ bbox` would call this a violation — a **false positive**.

**Design consequence for the gate (§5):** the FO-aware parser must key on overflow mode —
enforce box ⊆ bbox **only for `overflow:hidden` FOs** (pill labels, which *must* fit), and treat
`overflow:visible` FOs as plain-text parity (excluded from the extent, like `<text>` already is).
That is a one-attribute discriminator (`overflow="visible"` on the FO / `overflow:hidden` in the
div style), both already present in the emit.

---

## 4. Clip-vs-grow recommendation (with rendered visuals)

Rendered with local headless chromium against the real `.scriba-annot-label` style
(`600 11px ui-monospace`); each pill box is set ~22 % narrower than the label needs, to model a
per-machine font miss. White pill = the measured/reserved extent; text = what the browser paints.

<p align="center"><img alt="clip vs grow: (a) overflow:hidden clips mid-label; (b) overflow:visible overhangs past the white pill; (c) slack-widened box; reference short label fits" width="660" src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></p>

Reading the panels:

- **(a) current — `white-space:nowrap;overflow:hidden`.** Label is cut mid-token
  (`…min(dp[i-1][j]`). The honesty pin sees only the pill rect → green. **Silent data loss.**
- **(b) `overflow:visible` + nowrap.** Full label is readable but **spills past the white pill
  background** — reads as broken (text with no backing), and forces the extent story to budget an
  overhang on *every* pill (interaction the task flagged). Probe: the +13 px overhang of 2.2 is
  exactly this, escaping the FO-blind pin.
- **(c) `box_w = measured × (1+slack)`.** Widen `pill_w` **and** `box_w` together so the pill
  still backs the text. Helps — but at +30 % the label here **still clips**, because the estimate
  was wrong by *more* than the slack. Slack is a mitigation, not a guarantee, and it taxes the
  reserved lane on every math pill.
- **reference.** Short labels fit by construction — the common case no machine ever clips.

### Recommendation

1. **Root-cause fix (durable): pin the annotation-label font.** The reporter's "font not pinned"
   is literally true here — `estimate_text_width` runs the em-heuristic `_char_display_width`
   (`_text_render.py:94,77`), **not** the pinned "Scriba Sans" measurer (which covers 14 px
   cell/node text only; `_text_metrics.py` has no 11 px / annotation scope). Bundle the 11 px
   label font (as KaTeX and Scriba Sans are already bundled) and calibrate the estimate to its
   real advances. Then `box_w` is exact → no clip, no overhang, **and the rect-based honesty pin
   becomes truthful** for free. This is the real fix.
2. **Interim, until the font is pinned:** keep `overflow:hidden` (never overhang past the pill —
   panel (b) reads worse than a clip) with **symmetric `text-align:center`** so a miss trims both
   ends equally, plus a modest `box_w` slack (~12–15 %). Reject pure (a) (silent asymmetric clip)
   and pure (b) (overhang). This is panel (c)'s geometry with centered trimming — bounded,
   honest-looking, and it degrades gracefully rather than escaping the pill.
3. Pair either with the browser gate (§5.2) — it is the only check that sees the residual clip.

---

## 5. Gate design

### 5.1 Browserless — teach the extent parsers to ingest FO rects (fixes §2 + §3)

**Spec.** In both `_extent.py` (production) and `tests/helpers/painted_extent.py` (twin):
add `foreignObject` to `_TAG_RE`; on match, fold `x/y/width/height` (through the translate stack)
into the accumulator **iff** the FO is clip-mode. Clip-mode discriminator: the FO lacks
`overflow="visible"` **and** its div style contains `overflow:hidden`. `overflow:visible` FOs are
skipped (plain-`<text>` parity — prevents the metricplot false positive of §3.2).

**New test** `tests/unit/test_fo_within_bbox.py`: for a math-label battery, `emit_svg(
render_inline_tex=_fake_tex)` (the honesty tests must **opt in** to tex — this is the fix for the
emit-layer blindness of §2/3a) and assert each clip-mode FO box ⊆ `bounding_box()`. This catches
the over-sized/mis-placed box of 2.2 and gives the `0.01 px` pins teeth on the FO class.

**Cheap invariant (land first, zero churn):** assert `box_w == pill_w` and the per-line FO x/y
nest within the pill rect, directly in a unit test over `_emit_pill_label_text` output. This pins
the exact property that makes the escape dormant, with no parser change and no golden dependence.

**What browserless still can't do (be explicit):** it cannot detect the reported clip. `box_w :=
estimate_text_width(line) + pad`, so any CI check of "does `estimate_text_width(line)` fit
`box_w`?" is **tautologically true** (Probe 4a: every case `fits => True`). The estimator that
sizes the box cannot also judge the box. Only a real font advance differs — which needs a browser.

### 5.2 Opt-in browser probe — the only thing that catches the reported bug (manual battery)

**Spec** (`scripts/`-level, opt-in, not blocking CI): render each battery scene's real SVG with
the production CSS + KaTeX in headless chromium (present locally at `/opt/homebrew/bin/chromium`;
I used it for §4), then over `.scriba-annot-label` / `.scriba-annot-fobj`:

- **Clip:** flag `el.scrollWidth > el.clientWidth + 1` (text wider than its clip box).
- **Overhang:** flag `labelDiv.right > pillRect.right + 1` or `labelDiv.left < pillRect.left − 1`
  (guards option (b) style spill if it is ever adopted).

Emit a JSON report (scene, selector, `scrollWidth`, `clientWidth`, overflow px). Run it across
theme + a couple of representative system-font fallbacks to surface per-machine variance. This is
manual/opt-in precisely because it depends on browser + font availability that byte-golden CI
deliberately avoids.

---

## 6. Blast radius + landing order

**All 16 primitives** consume `annotation_height_above` / `_reserved_arrow_above`, so anything
that changes the *measured* buffer is scene-wide. The key de-risking fact from the probes:

- **Making the parsers FO-aware is inert for reservations today.** `_measure_emit` emits no FO
  (no tex; §2.1), so `annotation_height_above` never encounters an FO → **no reservation change →
  0 golden churn** across the 113 golden SVG/HTML files. `test_painted_within_bbox` likewise
  emits no tex, so it does not flip. New coverage lives in the **new** tex-opted-in test (§5.1).
- **Golden churn is opt-in and separable.** It occurs only if you additionally thread
  `render_inline_tex` into `_measure_emit` so math labels reserve by real FO/rect geometry — then
  lanes grow where math labels exist and goldens with math annotations churn. Gate that behind a
  **`SCRIBA_VERSION` 13 → 14** bump (the integer exists for exactly this — byte-different SVG,
  `_version.py:6`) and regolden in that commit alone.
- **Spec/doc updates:**
  - `_extent.py:13-17` and `painted_extent.py:5-7` — the exactness/"text is not measured / label
    sits inside its measured pill rect" claims need the FO caveat (true for stroke geometry;
    **not** for FO text under a font miss).
  - `docs/spec/svg-emitter.md` §"Layout envelope reservation (R-32)" — state that FO clip-boxes
    are now part of measured extent (and `overflow:visible` FOs are explicitly excluded).
  - `docs/spec/smart-label-ruleset.md` — add a rule ID for "FO clip-box ⊆ pill rect ⊆ bbox" and
    "math label text must fit its box (browser-verified)"; wire a conformance code into
    `docs/spec/error-codes.md` alongside the R-32 codes if the browser probe is promoted.

### Landing order

1. **FO-aware parsers (clip-mode only) + `box_w == pill_w` invariant test + tex-opted-in
   `test_fo_within_bbox`.** Inert on reservations, 0 golden churn, closes the §2.2 escape and
   gives the pins teeth. Ship first.
2. **Interim clip-vs-grow** (§4.2): `overflow:hidden` + centered trim + modest `box_w` slack —
   or jump straight to (3) if font-pinning is scheduled.
3. **Pin the 11 px label font** (§4.1) — the durable fix; makes 1–2 mostly moot and the rect pin
   truthful. Calibrate `estimate_text_width` to the bundled advances.
4. **Opt-in browser clip probe** in the manual battery (§5.2) — the only catcher of the residual
   clip; keep out of blocking CI.
5. **(Optional, churny)** thread `render_inline_tex` into `_measure_emit` so reservations are
   math-FO-aware → `SCRIBA_VERSION` 13 → 14 + regolden.
